tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, significant proteinuria, glucosuria, bilirubinuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting impaired renal function with possible nephrotic features. Low HbA1c and normal blood glucose argue against chronic hyperglycemia. Urine specific gravity is low, indicating impaired concentrating ability. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Findings are consistent with chronic kidney disease; further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
